column2: I wanted to go for more than ten pounds (upon leaving the exp) because I could have just put my desciion number as 1 but I wanted more so I went for a number that would be just maybe too high for peoples comfort in order to ensure I was in the top 2 (I think some people may haven chosen lower number because if they were in the bottom tier they didn't want that number subtracted from 5.73.
column3: I wanted to get at least 5£ and also the higher the number you pick the higher cost it has to you with no guarantee of being the highest payoff
column4: I decided that a number over 60 was required in order to be safe if the random number chosen was -60. In order to increase my chances of being one of the higher two earners I decided to choose a higher number, but not 125 as that cost amount (£6.94) would then lose me money as it would be subtracted from the 13.62. Therefore I chose a mid way between 60 and 125 of 117.
column5: Did the amount that would get me 10 pounds of profit. As some of the result was down to luck I wasn't too fussed about securing the higher amount
column6: Figured my choice was a somewhat safe option, as cost wasn't super high but the decision number still had a chance to be one of the highest two.
column7: i chose a higher number so even with a deduction it is at a decent cost. A higher number seemed like a safer option for me and similarly i chose numbers above a 20-30 range for guessing other participants answers.
column8: i picked a decision number that i perceive to have a low enough cost that when it gets substracted from the final, i won't lose as much. but i also made sure it wasn't very low. basically, i made my decisions based on the safest possible choice form my perspective. i thought other people in my group would do the same, but choose a higher number.
column9: i went with a number around 60 as the cost of my decision wasn't too damaging even if i got a lower number
column10: Trying to get in the top two scores in the group, without wasting too much possible payoff with higher decision numbers. Essentially, giving myself a chance of a payoff of around £10 at a cost of a potential actual payoff of £2, rather than accpeting a payoff of £5.73 by choosing 0 as the decision number.
column11: The difference between the higher pay off and the lower payoff is £7.89, so i was willing to pay up to that to try to ensure winning. The average effect of the random scores generated was 0, so i had no controll over that. I chose a high number to try to counter the effects of any random loss, and was willing to pay. The difference between the higher payoff at decision 125 and the lower payoff at decision 0 was £6.68, so \"winning at all costs\" was better than taking the lower option
column13: I thought other will choose 0 as it didnt take any risk so I choose the number sightly higher than 60. Howover, it seems people chose a higher number or they think as same as me and I am in bad luck.
